perturbation.original_text: “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship;
perturbation.changed_text: “Information” means only written confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, but excludes the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship;
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the definition of 'Information' in Section 1 to include ONLY written data, and exclude the fact of the agreement itself. This contradicts with Section 2a, which requires all information to be held in confidence. Now it's unclear if verbal information must be kept confidential, or if the existence of the agreement is confidential information, causing uncertainty in the enforcement of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Information' to include only written data and exclude the existence of the agreement. The contradicted text requires all 'Information' to be held in confidence. By excluding the existence of the agreement from the definition of 'Information', it creates uncertainty as to whether the fact that the parties entered into the agreement must be kept confidential, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency, as described in the Perturbation Explanation and adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.  The ETI shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the Respondents (or any of them) to: a. such of the ETI Affiliates, and either the ETI’s or the ETI Affiliates’ employees, officers, secondees, agent s, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, professional advisers and proposed professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose, provided that all such aforementioned persons to whom any Information is disclosed by th e ETI are bound by obligations of confidentiality and the ETI shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons and each ETI Affiliate may enforce this clause in accordance with the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999; and
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The ETI shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the Respondents (or any of them) to: a. such of the ETI Affiliates, and either the ETI’s or the ETI Affiliates’ employees, officers, secondees, agent s, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, professional advisers and proposed professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for any purpose, provided that all such aforementioned persons to whom any Information is disclosed by th e ETI are bound by obligations of confidentiality and the ETI shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons and each ETI Affiliate may enforce this clause in accordance with the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999; and
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies Section 5a, by changing 'where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose' to 'where such disclosure is necessary for any purpose'. This creates an in-text contradiction to the core concept of the NDA, which is to protect confidentiality ONLY for the stated purpose (defined in section 1). Now it's unclear whether disclosure is allowed for purposes outside of the stated purpose, potentially invalidating the NDA's intended function.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement, unless the context requires otherwise, the following words shall have the following meanings: “Disclosing Party” means any Party that discloses Information pursuant to this Agreement; “Effective Date” means the date of execut ion of this Agreement; “EoI” means the request for expressions of interest relating to the Project, issued by the ETI on 11th May 2016; “ETI Affiliates” means the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills (and any successor governmental depart ment or agency from time to time) and any other entity which is entitled to appoint the directors or otherwise having the ability to direct management policies of the ETI (together with any affiliates of those entities), together with their respective offi cers, employees, agents and consultants; “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship; “Project” means the proposed project under the ETI’s Bioenergy Programme entitled the “ Biomass Feedstock Improvement Process Project”; “Project Commissioning Process” means the ETI’s commissioning process for the Project as defined and as set out in the EoI or as later may be notified or published by the ETI; “Project Contract” means a Project Contract as such term is defined in the EoI; “Purpose” means: a. the preparation and/or submission of any Submission and related documents in response to the EoI; Non-Disclosure Agreement Energy Technologies Institute © 2016 Energy Technologies Institute LLP. The information in this document is the property of Energy Technologies Institute LLP and may not be copied or communicated to a third party or used for any purpose other than that for which it is supplied without the express written consent of Energy Technologies Institute LLP. www.eti.co.uk Delivering the UK’s Future Energy Technologies 3 b. the Project Commissioning Process; c. any activities related to the assessment of any Submissio n for the Project; and d. any related exchanges of Information, clarif ications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the EoI, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; and “Submission ” means a submission as such term is defined in the EoI; “Third Party IP” means any intellectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Submission .
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5a allows disclosure of information only 'where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose', aligning with the defined 'Purpose' in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text broadens this to 'where such disclosure is necessary for any purpose', which contradicts the NDA's core function of confidentiality specifically related to the defined 'Purpose'. This creates a direct conflict because the 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Information' as being disclosed 'in respect of or further to the Purpose'. Therefore, disclosing information for 'any purpose' now allows for disclosure outside the defined 'Purpose', directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' and the intention of the NDA.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, pro posed owners or lessors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by written ob ligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Information shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, pro posed owners or lessors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by verbal ob ligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Information shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies section 7 by changing 'written obligations' to 'verbal obligations'. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 2c, which requires taking 'security precautions' to safeguard information. Now it's ambiguous if verbal assurances are sufficient security precautions, potentially undermining the security of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of t he Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreeme nt; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for arc hival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 7, replacing 'written obligations' with 'verbal obligations'. This creates a direct conflict with section 2c, which mandates 'security precautions' to safeguard information. The explanation highlights that it becomes ambiguous whether verbal assurances are sufficient security precautions. Section 2c requires 'all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information)' which is clearly contradicted by merely verbal assurances being enough security precautions. This change therefore introduces a strong in-text contradiction because the modified clause introduces a weaker obligation (verbal) compared to the security precautions detailed in the contradicted text (written).

perturbation.original_text: 14. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date, or until either party provides written notice of termination, unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 14 adds a clause allowing termination with written notice. This directly contradicts the original seven-year duration of the NDA, creating an in-text contradiction. It's now unclear if the NDA is binding for seven years or can be terminated earlier, creating uncertainty in its enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date, or until either party provides written notice of termination, unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 14 introduces a termination clause with written notice, directly contradicting the original agreement's fixed seven-year duration. The contradicted text (which is oddly a repeat of the changed text, but that doesn't take away the underlying issue) emphasizes the uncertainty created: is the agreement for seven years, or can it be terminated earlier? This inconsistency, according to the explanation provided, creates an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: 16. The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not p reclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived verbally or in writing. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall preclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.explanation: Section 16 previously stated rights can be waived ONLY in writing. The change allows verbal waivers and states that delay in exercising a right constitutes a waiver. This creates an in-text contradiction, making the waiver requirements unclear. It becomes uncertain if a verbal agreement is enough to waive the rights, and if delay is considered as a waiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of r ights under the general law and may be waived verbally or in writing. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall preclude any further or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 16 introduces direct contradictions. The original text specifies that rights can be waived 'only in writing,' while the changed text allows for 'verbal or in writing' waivers, creating an immediate conflict. Furthermore, the original text states that delay in exercising a right is 'not a waiver,' but the changed text states that it 'is a waiver.' This direct conflict makes it uncertain how rights can be waived and if delays constitute a waiver, thus satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
